However, the IT sector continues to face selling pressure, with a neutral to bearish outlook. Investors are advised to watch for buying opportunities in the broader market and sell rallies in IT stocks.

stories.title: IFCI, HFCL among 14 stocks that rallied up to 50% in just one month - Do you own any?
stories.summary: While benchmark indices moved steadily, broader market momentum surged, with many BSE 500 stocks delivering double-digit gains, several rallying 25–50% in a month, highlighting strong stock-specific performance beneath calm markets.

stories.title: NSE flags regulatory, tech, AI, derivatives concentration risks in IPO papers
stories.summary: The National Stock Exchange raises a red flag regarding substantial financial and operational threats. Emerging regulatory frameworks, tech breakdowns, cyber threats, and the rise of AI present notable concerns. The NSE's dependency on derivatives revenue leaves it exposed. Recent adjustments from Sebi have already dampened trading volumes, and stricter regulations could further constrict both activity and profit margins.

stories.title: Extended summer may lift AC sales, but growth likely to fall short of expectations: Praveen Sahay
stories.summary: India's room air conditioner market anticipates a boost from an extended summer and El Niño, though growth is tempered by conservative dealer inventory stocking. While retail demand has been strong, lower primary sales to dealers have capped overall industry expansion, impacting expected growth figures.

stories.title: SBI to raise Rs 60,000 cr in FY27 via bonds
stories.summary: State Bank of India's board has approved raising up to Rs 60,000 crore in the current fiscal year through debt instruments. These funds will be raised via long-term bonds, Basel III-compliant Additional Tier 1, and Tier 2 Bonds. The issuance will be through public offer or private placement to Indian and overseas investors during FY27.

stories.title: Bitcoin trades near $64,000 despite hawkish Fed outlook; recovery attempt loses momentum
stories.summary: Bitcoin hovered near $64,000 as a hawkish Federal Reserve outlook and risk-off sentiment capped recovery momentum. Liquidations, ETF outflows and weak institutional participation weighed on prices. Key support lies between $61,000 and $63,500, while resistance near $67,500 remains a hurdle for any sustained upside in the near term.

stories.title: Smallcap stock jumps 24% in a week as NSE stake could be valued at Rs 850 crore
stories.summary: Maithan Alloys shares saw a significant jump on Thursday. This rally follows the filing of the National Stock Exchange's draft prospectus for its initial public offering. Maithan Alloys holds a stake in NSE, which is now valued at approximately Rs 850 crore. The NSE IPO is expected to be India's largest ever.

stories.title: Rupee hits six-week high on offshore, exporter flows
stories.summary: The Indian Rupee saw a remarkable resurgence on Thursday, hitting a six-week high after a period of weakness influenced by the Federal Reserve's strict monetary stance. This turnaround was fueled by increased dollar selling from foreign markets and robust exporter contributions. Furthermore, the recent downturn in oil prices offered additional support.

stories.title: Tulip Infratech to invest Rs 1,100 cr on construction of luxury housing project in Gurugram
stories.summary: Tulip Infratech is making a bold move with a staggering investment of Rs 1,100 crore for a new upscale residential venture in Gurugram, aptly named Tulip Melrose. This luxurious development will boast over 600 exquisite units located along the vibrant Southern Peripheral Road, with construction slated to unfold over the next four years.

stories.title: Rupee’s oil relief capped by RBI's FX book, interest payment hedges, bankers say
stories.summary: The Indian rupee's upward surge encounters some obstacles. As the Reserve Bank of India gradually dissolves a hefty foreign exchange forward book, banks are also preparing for interest payments on foreign currency holdings. These maneuvers are likely to soak up dollar inflows, which could limit the rupee's rise. Analysts predict a cautious outlook for the currency's future gains.

stories.title: Nykaa shares jump 8% to fresh 52-week high after unveiling FY30 roadmap; targets up to 3x revenue growth

stories.title: Japan's Nikkei gauge crosses 71,000 on US-Iran peace deal

The U.S. Federal Reserve's stance also influenced markets. Chip-related and financial stocks led the gains. Marine transportation stocks saw a decline. The Nikkei 225 index reached an intraday high of 71,293.64.

stories.title: Infosys, TCS, Wipro, other IT stocks fall up to 3% after Fed's hawkish tone. What's ahead?
stories.summary: Indian IT stocks, including Infosys and TCS, experienced a significant decline of up to 3% on Thursday. This downturn was triggered by a hawkish stance from the US Federal Reserve, which has increased expectations of a rate hike later this year. Concerns over reduced discretionary spending in North America, a key market for these companies, are driving the sell-off.

stories.title: Stay invested, keep accumulating quality stocks: Neeraj Dewan

Investors are advised to use market dips as buying opportunities, especially in mid and small-cap segments. While inflation and monsoon concerns persist, they are seen as chances to invest.

stories.title: Robinhood stock soars despite layoffs – Why Wall Street is bullish

stories.title: Archaeologists discover 5,000-year-old monument in Wiltshire linked to early Stonehenge traditions
stories.summary: A remarkable 5,000-year-old wooden monument, predating Stonehenge by 500 years, has been unearthed near Bulford, Wiltshire. This discovery, featuring two large posts aligned with the midsummer sunrise and midwinter sunset, suggests sophisticated ancient astronomy and ceremonial traditions were already established. Researchers believe this site may have inspired the later construction of Stonehenge, offering crucial insights into Neolithic communities.

stories.title: German proverb of the day: 'Crooked logs also make straight fires'
stories.summary: Germany's adage 'crooked logs make straight fires' conveys a powerful message about human potential, reminding us that flaws do not detract from one's worth. It encourages us to utilize what we have rather than wait for perfect conditions, advocating against the tyranny of perfectionism.
